prompt_reports.prompt: A simple React landing page with a hero section, a call-to-action button, and a features section.
prompt_reports.judge_rationale: The spec fully covers all three requested elements: a hero section (heading, body text), a call-to-action button ("Get Started"), and a features section (3-column grid of feature cards). It goes slightly beyond the intent with an extra "Why Choose Us" section and an alert banner, but the core requir

prompt_reports.prompt: Build a full landing page with a hero section, feature grid, pricing table, testimonial carousel, and CTA.
prompt_reports.judge_rationale: The spec covers all five requested sections — hero, feature grid, pricing table, testimonials, and CTA — with rich, well-structured content. The only notable gap is that the testimonial section uses a static vertical stack of cards rather than a carousel component, which was explicitly requested.

prompt_reports.prompt: Build a user profile page showing: profile photo, display name, username, email, bio, member since date, activity stats (posts, comments, followers), recent activity feed with timestamps, edit profile and settings buttons.
prompt_reports.judge_rationale: The spec covers nearly all requested elements: profile photo, display name, username, email, bio, member since date, activity stats (posts, followers — though "comments" is replaced with "following"), recent activity feed with timestamps, and edit profile/settings buttons. prompt_reports.prompt: Build a support ticket dashboard. Shows: open tickets, response time, agent performance, recent activity. Mobile-first design (team leads check this on phones while on the floor). Light theme, high contrast. Color code: red for urgent (>24h), yellow for medium, green for on-time. Maximum 3-column layout. Include loading states for real-time data.
prompt_reports.judge_rationale: The spec covers the core requirements well: open tickets, response time, agent performance table, and recent activity feed with color-coded urgency badges. However, it falls short on several key points: the layout uses a 2-column grid (not mobile-first with up to 3 columns), there are no explicit lo

prompt_reports.prompt: Dashboard displaying: top 5 performers with names and revenue, team revenue vs quota progress bar, deal pipeline with stages (Leads → Qualified → Demo → Closed), 6-month revenue trend chart.
prompt_reports.judge_rationale: The spec covers top 5 performers (table with names and revenue) and the 6-month revenue trend, but uses stacked cards instead of a proper chart for the trend. The deal pipeline stages don't match the requested flow (Leads → Qualified → Demo → Closed), and the team revenue vs quota is shown as a stat
